biodynamic.name: Weingut Melsheimer
biodynamic.address: Dorfstrasse 21, 56861 Reil an der Mosel, Germany
biodynamic.description: Reil estate organic from the 1990s, fully biodynamic from 2009 and Demeter-certified in 2013, working the steep Mullay-Hofberg slate by hand for pure Riesling.
biodynamic.tip: The long-skin-contact Vade Retro bottlings show the biodynamic fruit at its most expressive; the classic Mullay-Hofberg Rieslings are the calling card.

biodynamic.name: Weingut Clemens Busch
biodynamic.description: Biodynamic since 2005, the Busch family farm the Puenderich Marienburg by respekt-BIODYN guidelines and bottle its slate parcels separately as a terroir study.
biodynamic.tip: The grey-, blue- and red-slate Marienburg GGs read the same biodynamic farming across three soils; taste them together.

organic.name: Weingut Weiser-Kuenstler
organic.description: Konstantin Weiser and Alexandra Kuenstler hand-farm the steep Trarbacher Schlossberg with no herbicides, their main organic-certified sites giving clear Riesling.
organic.tip: Tiny production and cult demand mean the organic-certified Schlossberg Kabinett sells out fast; buy through the importer on release.

natural.name: Weingut Rita und Rudolf Trossen
natural.description: Natural-wine pioneers in Kinheim since 1976, the Trossens make slate Riesling with no added yeast; from 2010 the Pyramide Purus line uses no added sulphur.
natural.tip: The Pyramide Purus Rieslings are the zero-sulphur cuvees; start with a classic bottling to read the slate before the wild ferment.

natural.name: Weingut Stein
natural.description: Ulli Stein farms steep slate ecologically with cover crops and no herbicides; his light, dry slate Riesling and rare Elbling are natural-wine-scene darlings.
natural.tip: Stein's wines are built for the table; the Elbling and steep St. Aldegunder Rieslings drink best young and lightly chilled.

vegan_winemaking.name: Weingut Dr. Loosen
vegan_winemaking.description: Dr. Loosen's Rieslings are vegan-friendly, with no animal products and bentonite-clay fining where needed, one of the most widely available vegan Mosel ranges.
vegan_winemaking.tip: The entry Dr. L and the estate Rieslings are all vegan-friendly, an easy, affordable choice for plant-based drinkers exploring the Mosel.

lowsulfite.name: Trossen Pyramide Purus
lowsulfite.description: The Trossens' Pyramide Purus Rieslings, made since 2010, are bottled with no filtration or sulfur addition, a benchmark of no-sulphur natural Mosel wine.
lowsulfite.tip: Zero-sulphur wines are alive and variable; store cold and drink Purus on the younger side for its purest slate-and-orchard expression.
